verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is a member of the EGF family synthesized as a ~19 kDa single-pass transmembrane proform (proepiregulin). The ectodomain is shed by ADAM metalloproteinases to yield the mature ~6 kDa soluble ligand that acts on EGFR and ERBB4. The TM precursor stage is real and supports juxtacrine signaling, but it is short-lived: the shed soluble form is the dominant biological actor. This is the canonical transient-TM-proligand architecture shared by TGFα, amphiregulin, HB-EGF, etc. The protein is not secreted_only (a TM stage exists), but the stable surface form is not the dominant state, placing it in contextual/dual_localization.
